etymology.description: 蔬(蔬) (shū, "vegetables") refers generally to edible plant parts like stems, leaves, and other non-fruit elements. It was traditionally associated with garden or cultivated plants. 菜(菜) (cài, "vegetable; dish") represents both the edible plants themselves and prepared dishes. In ancient China, 菜(菜) often referred to vegetables served as a dish in meals.
etymology.interpretation: The term 蔬菜(蔬菜) (shūcài, "vegetables") combines the notion of edible plants with both their raw form and their role as ingredients in cooking, emphasizing their importance in Chinese culinary tradition.

examples.chinese: 春天到了(春天到了)，花园里的蔬菜长得很快(花園裡的蔬菜長得很快)。
examples.pinyin: Chūntiān dàole, huāyuán lǐ de shūcài zhǎng de hěn kuài.
examples.english: In spring, the vegetables in the garden grow quickly.

examples.chinese: 孩子们应该多吃蔬菜(孩子們應該多吃蔬菜)。
